question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the service_endpoints JSONB column returned by the native query to observe internal IP addresses and port configurations of block nodes, mirror nodes, and RPC relays, and use this information to target specific infrastructure nodes for attacks that would reorganize the block/transaction history at the network level?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the admin_key field returned in the API response (as a hex-encoded byte array) to enumerate the public keys of all registered node administrators, and use this information to identify which nodes are controlled by the same key, enabling targeted attacks to reorganize transaction history by compromising a key that controls multiple nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the created_timestamp field exposed in the API response to correlate registered node creation events with specific consensus transactions, and by comparing the created_timestamp ordering with the registered_node_id ordering, detect cases where the importer processed transactions out of order, indicating a history reorganization vulnerability in the importer pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the timestamp.from and timestamp.to fields in the API response to identify nodes whose timestamp_range has an open upper bound (to=null), and by comparing these with nodes that have a closed upper bound in registered_node_history, detect inconsistencies in how the importer recorded the history of node state transitions, effectively revealing history reorganization artifacts?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the type filter combined with a narrow registerednode.id range to perform a binary search across all registered node IDs, enumerating the complete set of registered nodes and their types in O(log N) requests, and use this map to identify which nodes are block nodes versus mirror nodes to target for history reorganization attacks?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the native query uses 'select *' to return all columns including service_endpoints JSONB, causing the API to expose the full endpoint configuration of every registered node in a single paginated traversal, providing an attacker with a complete map of the network infrastructure needed to plan a history reorganization attack?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of rate limiting on the GET /api/v1/network/registered-nodes endpoint to send thousands of requests with varying lowerBound/upperBound values, causing a denial-of-service condition that prevents legitimate clients from querying the registered node history and effectively reorganizing the availability of historical data?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the MAX_LIMIT value for the limit parameter to cause the native query to fetch a very large number of rows from registered_node in a single database transaction, causing excessive memory consumption and potentially causing the database to return a stale or partial snapshot of the registered node history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Spring Data JPA native query mechanism by supplying a registerednode.id parameter value that causes the :lowerBound or :upperBound named parameter binding to fail silently, causing the query to use a default value (such as 0 or null) that returns an unintended set of nodes and reorganizes the visible history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the PostgreSQL array containment operator '@>' in the native query by passing a type value that corresponds to a Short value not defined in RegisteredNodeType enum, causing the query to match nodes whose type array contains that raw numeric value and exposing nodes that are not visible through the normal type filter, reorganizing the visible node history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the Pageable object passed to the native query always uses page number 0 (PageRequest.of(0, request.getLimit(), sort)) regardless of the registerednode.id cursor, causing the query to always return the first page of results relative to the lowerBound, and by manipulating lowerBound, simulate arbitrary pagination that could skip or duplicate nodes in the history view?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the combination of order=desc and a registerednode.id=lt:X parameter to cause the query to return nodes in descending order starting from X-1, and by comparing this with an ascending query from 0 to X-1, detect any nodes that appear in one ordering but not the other due to concurrent modifications, revealing history reorganization events?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the description field (varchar(100)) returned in the API response to observe changes in node descriptions across paginated requests, and by comparing description values with those in registered_node_history (if accessible), detect cases where the current state does not match the expected state after a sequence of update transactions, indicating a history reorganization in the importer?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the mergeRegisteredNode() logic in SqlEntityListener where null fields in an update are preserved from the previous state, by observing the API response for nodes that should have had their description or serviceEndpoints cleared by an update transaction, and detecting cases where the merge incorrectly preserved old values, causing the history to appear reorganized?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the blockNodeForRest() method in RegisteredNodeMapper that returns an empty map for any non-null BlockNodeEndpoint, causing the API to hide the actual endpoint_apis details of block nodes, and use this information gap to identify which block nodes have been updated (their endpoint_apis changed) without the change being visible in the API response, effectively reorganizing the observable history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the mapRegisteredNodeType() default case in RegisteredNodeMapper that maps any unrecognized Short value to GENERAL_SERVICE, causing nodes with future or unknown type values to be misclassified in the API response, and by filtering on type=GENERAL_SERVICE, retrieve nodes that should not be visible under that type, reorganizing the type-based history view?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the int8range timestamp_range column returned in the API response as timestamp.from/timestamp.to to identify nodes whose timestamp_range lower endpoint is earlier than their created_timestamp, indicating an inconsistency in how the importer recorded the node creation event, which could be used to argue that the transaction history has been reorganized?
